original_snippet_context: Analysis of the term "Presidente de Moraes" in the context of early 20th-century Brazilian monetary debates.
justification: While "Presidente de Moraes" likely refers to Prudente José de Morais Barros, the 3rd President of Brazil (1894-1898), his presidency predates the 1906 Caixa de Conversão debates.  The mention itself doesn't offer any stance on the monetary policy in question.
